page.title: Tour Guide Via Baltica — Mark Jeziak, Gdańsk
page.description: Private guided tours in Gdańsk with certified local guide Mark Jeziak. English & Polish. Book your Baltic Sea city experience today.
hero.heading:
Discover Gdańsk
like a local
hero.body: Private guided tours through one of Europe's most beautiful Baltic cities — where history, amber and the sea come together.
about.heading: A passion for stories, a love for Gdańsk.
about.p1: I'm Mark — born and raised in Gdańsk, certified by the Polish Tourist Organisation, and lucky enough to spend my days walking visitors through the city I grew up in. For nearly two decades I've been turning Gdańsk's tangled history of Hanseatic merchants, Teutonic knights, amber traders and shipyard strikes into stories you actually remember.
about.p2: My tours are private and slow on purpose. We linger in courtyards, stop at the bakeries I'd take my own family to, and skip the gift-shop loop that most coach tours run. Whether it's two of you on a long weekend or twelve of you celebrating something — I'd love to show you around.
about.placeholder: Replace with a photo of Mark on location
about.h2desc: Native Polish, fluent English. German and French on request.
about.h3desc: 40 years in Gdańsk. Every courtyard, legend and shortcut.
about.h4desc: 18 reviews, all five stars. Read what travellers say below.

trips.cancellation_note: No upfront payment — confirmation by email.
blog.heading: Stories from Gdańsk
faq.title: Common questions
faq.subtitle: A few things travellers usually ask before they book. Anything else, just write.
faq.a1: Tours are priced per group, not per person — so a couple pays the same as a family of five. Listed prices are starting points for groups of up to 4; larger groups and longer routes can be quoted on request. Children under 12 are free.
faq.a2: Cancel for any reason up to 48 hours before the tour for a full refund. Weather cancellations are at my discretion and always free of charge — we can reschedule or refund.
faq.q3: Where do tours start and end?
faq.a3: Most tours start at Neptune Fountain in the Long Market, or I can meet you at your hotel anywhere in central Gdańsk, Sopot or Gdynia. Coast and day-trip tours include hotel pickup.
faq.q4: What languages do you guide in?
faq.a4: English and Polish are my native working languages. I can also guide in German or French on request — please mention it when booking.
faq.a5: Old Town tours involve cobblestones and a few stairs but are otherwise gentle and easy to pace. The family-friendly route is fully stroller-accessible. Just tell me your group's needs and we'll tailor the walk.
faq.a6: Always. About a third of my bookings are custom — themed around food, photography, Solidarity-era history, or family roots. Tell me what you're interested in when you write.
reviews.heading: Trusted by travellers
reviews.r1.text: Mark is an exceptional guide — knowledgeable, passionate, and incredibly engaging. The Via Baltica tour exceeded every expectation. Highly recommended!
reviews.r2.text: Absolutely fantastic experience in Gdańsk. Mark brings history to life in a way no textbook ever could. We'll be back for the next tour!
reviews.r3.text: One of the best guided experiences I've had in Europe. The route along Via Baltica was perfectly curated, and Mark's storytelling made every stop memorable.
